message.content:
Here are some brands that are consistently considered reliable for **regular home garden use** — meaning good durability, reasonable price, and solid performance without being overly “pro‑grade” or expensive.

**Hand tools (trowels, pruners, loppers, etc.):**
• Fiskars – Very reliable, long-lasting, great pruners and shears  
• Corona – Excellent cutting tools, often better than budget brands  
• Felco – Professional quality pruners; pricey but last for decades  
• Spear & Jackson – Good balance of price and durability  
• Burgon & Ball – High-quality British tools, especially hand tools  

**Lawn and power tools (mowers, trimmers, blowers):**
• Honda – Best for gas mowers, extremely long-lasting  
• Toro – Reliable mowers for home use  
• EGO – Top-rated battery-powered outdoor tools; great runtime  
• Ryobi – Affordable and dependable for homeowners; huge battery ecosystem  
• Greenworks – Solid, budget-friendly battery-powered tools  

**Shovels, rakes, and long-handled tools:**
• True Temper – Classic, durable, widely available  
• Bully Tools – Very tough, made in USA, great for heavier work  
• Ames – Good quality for everyday home gardening  

**Watering tools (hoses, sprinklers, wands):**
• Gardena – Extremely reliable and well-designed  
• Dramm – Excellent nozzles and wands, heavy-duty  
• Flexzilla – Great lightweight hoses that resist kinking  

If you want, tell me your general garden size and needs (flowers, lawn care, veggie beds, pruning shrubs, etc.), and I can recommend a more targeted starter set.
